hypothesis: If the Segment Anything Model 2 is fine-tuned on physics-informed synthetic LPTEM videos spanning realistic liquid thickness/SNR variability with box prompts, then it will segment and track nanoparticles in LPTEM videos in real time with higher IoU/F1 (J&F) than a U-Net baseline across particle counts, overlaps, and resolutions, and yield centroid trajectories that agree with manual annotations on real data because foundation-model priors combined with diverse, physically realistic training confer robustness to low-SNR conditions.
